title: B.A.C.A. OF OREGON - 211info
text: BIKERS AGAINST CHILD ABUSE B.A.C.A. OF OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Supports children at court, parole hearings or attending case interviews by trained biker volunteers. Volunteer opportunities are available. Eligibility: Ages 3-18 with a reported incident of sexually, physical, or emotional child abuse Volunteers: Age 18 and older, with regular access to a motorcycle, and the ability to pass a complete background check. Additional requirements may vary on level of participation. Hours: No standard hours Email (971) 259-8618 Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (971) 259-8618 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Visit website, call, or email for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake AREA SERVED: State of Oregon Services: Crime Victim Accompaniment Services * Child Abuse Issues Volunteer Opportunities * Child Abuse Issues If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. No Mapping Information Available
